title: Oil prices surge past $118 per barrel on Hormuz blockade tensions

contents:
# Oil prices surge past $118 per barrel on Hormuz blockade tensions

## Overview
Global oil prices rallied sharply on Wednesday with Brent crude closing above $118 per barrel due to fears of extended Strait of Hormuz disruptions. The surge followed U.S. President Trump's signal to continue the naval blockade of Iran until a nuclear deal remains reached.

## Details
Trump directed aides to prepare for prolonged blockade of Iranian ports to pressure Tehran's economy. The Strait normally carries one-fifth of global oil and LNG supplies. Iran restricted most non-Iranian shipping since late February after U.S. and Israeli actions on Feb. 28. U.S. countered by blockading Iranian-linked vessels. Wall Street Journal reported lower risks in blockade versus bombings. Negotiations remain stalled with Iran claiming alternative routes.

**Location**: Strait of Hormuz

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @US5thFleet, which covers US 5th Fleet Strait of Hormuz Iran IRGC Houthi Red Sea. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following US 5th Fleet Strait of Hormuz Iran IRGC Houthi Red Sea will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
